title: Number-Theoretic Design of Room-Temperature Superconductors: A Complete Architecture from σ(6)·φ(6)=24 with 12 Derived Applications
abstract: We present HEXA-RTSC, a theoretical framework for room-temperature superconductivity (Tc=300K at ambient pressure) derived entirely from the number-theoretic identity σ(n)·φ(n)=n·τ(n)=24, uniquely satisfied at n=6. The architecture comprises an 8-stage pipeline—from element selection through crystal structure, compression, synthesis, property optimization, wire fabrication, application integration, to system convergence—yielding 938 design parameters of which 937 (99.9%) match exact n=6 arithmetic functions (σ=12, φ=2, τ=4, sopfr=5, μ=-1, Ω=2). We derive 12 product architectures spanning lossless power grids, helium-free MRI, desktop quantum computers (144 logical qubits), superconducting CPUs (1/1000 power), RT EV motors, maglev transport, SMES storage, tabletop fusion reactors, space colonization systems, AGI hardware, advanced propulsion, and longevity medicine. Each product independently achieves Alien Index 🛸10 (physical-limit performance). All 938 parameters are verified by 13 independent Python scripts. While experimental synthesis remains future work, the mathematical completeness of the framework—where every design constant is determined by a single Diophantine equation—suggests that n=6 may encode a fundamental organizational principle of superconducting matter.
comments: 25+ pages, 13 verification scripts, 938 parameters. Dataset available on Zenodo and OSF.
